summary: DHH highlights early converts to Ruby on Rails, quoting blogger Jogin who explains choosing Ruby over Python specifically because of Rails. The post captures a pivotal moment in Rails' early adoption where developers were switching languages not for Ruby itself, but for the framework built on top of it. DHH frames this as validation of Rails' appeal, noting multiple people independently making the same language switch.
key_points: Developers were choosing Ruby over Python specifically because of Rails, not because of language preference alone
Ruby's deep object orientation was seen as superior to Python's 'afterthought' OO, but that alone wasn't enough to tip the scales
Rails was becoming a gravitational force pulling developers from other language communities as early as 2004
money_quotes: I'm willing to code Ruby, even though I prefer Python, because of Rails.
A lot of people are moving over to Ruby because they want to play with Rails.
Python's OO feels kind of like an afterthought, most likely because it is one.
What settles the matter is Rails.
key_insight: Rails was so compelling in 2004 that developers were willing to switch programming languages entirely just to use it, marking the framework as a stronger draw than the language itself.
